additions.template: what did i write in my journal
what have i written in my journal
set a goal to run {target:quantity} kilometers
set a goal to run {target:quantity}k this month
set a goal to run {target:quantity} km this month
my goal is to run {target:quantity} km
my goal is to run {target:quantity}k this month
my goal is to run {target:quantity} km this month
set a running goal of {target:quantity} km
set my running goal to {target:quantity} km
set a monthly goal to run {target:quantity}k
set a monthly goal to run {target:quantity} km
i want to run {target:quantity}k this month
i want to run {target:quantity} km this month
am i on track for my running goal
how close am i to my goal
how close am i to my running goal
where am i on my goal
where am i with my goal
how far along am i on my goal
how much more do i need to run
how much is left on my goal

searchRegex.note: ^(?:can you |could you |please )?(?:(?:find|show(?: me)?|pull up|bring up|dig up|look up|look for|locate|do i have|is there|where(?:'?s| is| are))\s+(?:(?:that|my|the|a|an|any|all|old)\s+)*(?:notes?|entry|entries|journal(?:\s+entr(?:y|ies))?|thing|one)\s+(?:about|on|mentioning|regarding|where|that\s+(?:says?|mentions?))|where\s+did\s+i\s+(?:write|journal|note)\s+(?:something\s+)?(?:about|down)|what\s+did\s+i\s+(?:write|note)\s+(?:down\s+)?about)\s+(.+?)\??$
searchRegex.rationale: Both patterns keep the query as group(1), matching the existing `searchM.group(1)` call site in session.dart. _searchNoteRe gains: polite prefixes (can you/could you/please), more verbs (bring up, dig up, look up, look for, locate, do i have, is there), stacked determiners (find any of THE OLD notes... via repeated determiner group), and two new noun-less shapes anchored on first person writing verbs — 'where did i write about/down X' and 'what did i write about X' — which cannot over-match because they require 'did i write/journal/note'. _searchForRe gains: 'search through my notes/journal/entries for X', 'look/go/dig/flip through my notes for X', 'check/scan my notes for X' (verb other than 'search' REQUIRES the notes/journal noun, so 'check my calendar for tomorrow' does not match), and trailing-locative 'find X in my notes' / 'look up X in my journal' guarded by a lookahead so bare 'find my keys' cannot match; the '(in my notes)' tail is stripped from the captured query by the optional suffix group. Tested 27 positive and 12 negative utterances (all pass, negatives include 'where is my phone', 'find my keys', 'look up the weather', 'check my calendar for tomorrow', 'go through the plan'). Note: bare 'search X' matching anything after the word 'search' is pre-existing behavior, deliberately preserved.

gaps.want: non-running goals — 'set a goal to walk 10000 steps', 'goal: read 12 books', 'save $500 this month'
gaps.needs: set-goal skill hardcodes activity 'run' and km in its DSL (writes activity:'run', concat 'run {target}km'); needs an activity slot + unit field, and goal-progress hardcodes read_one match activity:'run' and sums workout.distance

gaps.want: goals in miles — 'set a goal to run 30 miles'
gaps.needs: unit slot or mi->km conversion in set-goal DSL; corpus quantity slot alone would silently store miles as km
goal-progress hardcodes start_of_month; needs a period field on goal and matching window compute

gaps.want: update or clear a goal — 'change my goal to 60k', 'delete my running goal'
gaps.needs: set-goal always write_record's a new goal; read_one on activity:'run' may pick a stale one — needs upsert or a delete-goal skill

gaps.want: recall journal by date — 'what did i write yesterday', 'journal entries from last week'
gaps.needs: recall-journal has no inputs; needs an optional {when:date} slot and a filter on loggedAt

gaps.want: read just the latest entry — 'read my last journal entry'
gaps.needs: recall-journal reads all entries; needs a limit/sort variant or a recall-latest-journal skill

gaps.want: delete/edit a journal entry — 'delete my last journal entry'
gaps.needs: no skill writes deletions of journal_entry; new skill + DSL delete op

gaps.want: search notes by date — 'find notes from last week', 'what did i write about sam in march'
gaps.needs: _searchContent is keyword/semantic only; date-scoped search needs a date filter parsed out of the query

gaps.want: diary-style openers without an anchor word — 'today i had a rough day'
gaps.needs: intentionally NOT added: bare 'today i {text}' over-matches loggable statements ('today i ran 5k', 'today i ate a sandwich') that belong to log-run/log-meal; safe only with a model-side disambiguator

notes: All templates are lowercase to match existing corpus style. log-journal additions stay anchored on journal/diary/write down/jot down/note down per over-match guidance; generic 'journal {entry:text}' was deliberately omitted because it would race the existing 'journal that {entry:text}' template and capture 'that ...' or 'entry ...' into the text slot if the router does not prefer longer literal matches. set-goal additions keep the goal/target anchor except the two 'i want to run Xk this month' forms, where the future-desire framing plus 'this month' makes over-match with log-run ('i ran 5k') implausible. 'am i on track' and 'goal progress' are bare but there is only one goal concept in the system today; revisit if multi-goal lands. searchRegex patterns were validated in a .NET regex harness (Dart RegExp shares the relevant syntax: non-capturing groups, lookahead, lazy quantifiers) with case-insensitive matching, mirroring caseSensitive:false in session.dart.
